title: Mean Reversion After Overextension Inside Bull Trend -- frozen strategy spec (pre-registration)

must_fix_s26_weaknesses: entry edge inconclusive -> entry is a DISCRETE two-step event (overextension THEN one-bar reversal) that can be significance-tested against random-in-regime entries
thin IS edge -> conservative capped design with a real PF gate (>=1.30)
2014-2016 shared NQ/ES chop collapse -> mean reversion is counter-cyclical to chop; regime breakdown (D9) will check it explicitly
OOS single-year dominance -> positive-years and top-3 winner gates
friction sensitivity -> friction gate (D12) with explicit R-cost thresholds
partial independent support -> ES used as the independent confirmation market (D10), never folded into the entry
design_context.must_avoid_donchian_winner_dependence: Donchian (S24 ENTRY_EDGE_NOT_SUPPORTED, S25 SEQUENCE_RISK_FRAGILE) made its money from a few fat-tail trend-capture winners -- removing the top 3 trades flipped IS and OOS negative. S27 deliberately uses a CAPPED target (+1.5R) so per-trade outcomes are bounded and repeatable rather than dependent on rare mega-winners, and adds a HARD gate: IS net must remain POSITIVE after removing the top 3 winners.

spec.1_strategy_name: Mean Reversion After Overextension Inside Bull Trend

2_market_and_timeframe.independent_confirmation_later: ES daily (D10 only; never part of the entry)
2_market_and_timeframe.OOS: 2023-2025 (SEALED until the OOS protocol step is committed)

spec.3_core_hypothesis: In a confirmed bull trend, sharp short-term overextensions to the downside tend to mean-revert over the next several bars. The edge must come from the DISCRETE overextension-then-reversal event, not from broad trend drift -- which is exactly why the entry is built to be significance-testable against random bull-trend entries.

4_bull_trend_filter.why_slope_included: Falling-knife risk in a rolling-over top is the #1 failure mode of buying dips. Requiring a non-negative EMA200 slope restricts entries to a genuinely RISING trend, not a topping market. It is a fixed, mechanical, binary gate -- no optimization, no sweep -- and directly tightens the otherwise-loose bull filter.

all_must_hold_on_signal_bar: close <= EMA20 - 1.0 * ATR20  (close is at least 1.0 ATR20 below EMA20)
close is the LOWEST close of the last 5 bars (current bar inclusive)
5_overextension_trigger.rationale: A single, fully mechanical overextension definition. Each clause is measurable and reproducible; no discretionary support/resistance drawing.

6_reversal_confirmation.rule: After the overextension trigger on bar i, require close[i+1] > close[i]. If satisfied, the setup is confirmed on bar i+1. If close[i+1] <= close[i], the setup is ABANDONED (expires; no entry).
6_reversal_confirmation.why_B_over_A: Option B gives (1) less falling-knife risk -- we wait for one up-bar before committing, and (2) cleaner entry-significance testing -- the entry is a discrete, datable TWO-STEP event (overextension THEN reversal) that can be compared against random entries drawn from the same bull-trend bars to isolate the trigger's marginal edge.

7_entry_timing.entry: next bar open = open of bar i+2
7_entry_timing.expiry: if confirmation fails at i+1, no entry; a new signal must form fresh

8_stop.rule: stop = entry - 1.5 * ATR20 (ATR20 as of the signal bar i)
8_stop.R_unit: 1R = 1.5 * ATR20 = the stop distance. All results in R only.
8_stop.why_1.5_ATR: Tighter than S26's 2N because mean-reversion entries are taken near a local extreme right after a reversal bar, so the invalidation level (a fresh low below the reversal) is closer. 1.5*ATR still leaves friction headroom comparable to S26's 2N.

9_exit.time_stop: exit at the CLOSE of the 10th bar held if neither stop nor target hit
9_exit.ema20_reclaim_exit: NOT used in v1 (kept out to keep the exit fixed and simple)
9_exit.exit_precedence: stop -> target -> time_stop. If stop and target are both touched on the same bar, STOP fills first (conservative).
9_exit.why_capped_target: A fixed +1.5R cap makes per-trade outcomes bounded and repeatable, deliberately avoiding Donchian's fat-tail-winner dependence.

12_pass_watch_fail_thresholds.note: Pre-registered BEFORE any results. Conservative. PASS = all met; WATCH = minor misses; FAIL = any hard floor breached.

max_dd_cap.pass: IS max DD <= IS net R
max_dd_cap.fail: IS max DD > 1.5x IS net R

top3_winner_dependence_cap.pass: IS net stays POSITIVE after removing top 3 winners
top3_winner_dependence_cap.fail: IS net goes negative without top 3 (Donchian failure mode)

friction_requirement.pass: IS positive at 0.05R AND OOS positive at 0.10R per trade

13_anti_overfit_rules: no changing RSI/ATR/EMA/target/stop/slope/time-stop after seeing results
if any rule changes, open a NEW branch (do not mutate S27 in place)
no OOS peeking before the OOS protocol is committed
no paper/live promotion from this spec or from IS results
14_expected_failure_modes: falling-knife risk (dips inside a rolling-over trend) -- mitigated by EMA200 slope gate + reversal confirmation + R-stop
bull filter too loose -- mitigated by the slope gate
signal count too low (two-step confirmation is restrictive) -- watch IS/OOS trade floors
target too optimistic (+1.5R rarely reached on snap-backs) -- watch win rate vs time-stop exits
choppy-regime false positives (oversold keeps going) -- D9 regime check
friction sensitivity on a tighter 1.5*ATR stop -- D12 friction stress
spec.15_final_line: S27-D2 is a frozen hypothesis specification only; no backtest, OOS, paper, or live trading is authorized.

trading_recommendation: NONE. Frozen spec only. S26 and Donchian stay PARKED; S27 is an unbuilt, unvalidated hypothesis.
